novel_features.name: Growth Summary
novel_features.description: Summarize publication, subscriber, post, referral, and custom-field health in one read-only response.
novel_features.rationale: Combines several Beehiiv resources that otherwise require separate publication, subscription, post, referral, and field calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this first when an agent needs a compact account-level picture before choosing a narrower Beehiiv endpoint.

novel_features.name: Subscriber Sources
novel_features.description: Group subscribers by UTM, channel, and referring-site fields to see where audience growth is coming from.
novel_features.rationale: Turns raw subscription rows into source attribution counts without forcing an agent to page and aggregate manually.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the question is about acquisition channels rather than individual subscribers.

novel_features.name: Post Performance
novel_features.description: List recent posts with status, audience, publish timing, and any available expanded stats.
novel_features.rationale: Normalizes post metadata into a compact performance review surface for agents comparing recent sends.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to inspect content output before drilling into one post.

novel_features.name: Field Coverage
novel_features.description: Inspect custom-field definitions alongside subscriber sample size for enrichment planning.
novel_features.rationale: Pairs schema-level custom fields with audience sample context so agents can decide whether enrichment work is worthwhile.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before importing, enriching, or auditing subscriber metadata.

novel_features.name: Referral Health
novel_features.description: Summarize referral-program configuration and subscriber referral-code coverage.
novel_features.rationale: Connects publication-level referral settings with subscriber-level referral-code presence in one check.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to check whether referral growth is configured and visible in subscriber data.

novel_features.name: Subscriber Lookup
novel_features.description: Find one subscriber by email or subscription ID and return a compact subscriber record.
novel_features.rationale: Provides a safer single-record lookup surface before agents consider broader subscription exports.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the task is about one subscriber and broad list calls would waste context.

narrative.headline: beehiiv CLI with read-only growth, audience, post, field, and referral intelligence.
narrative.value_prop: Wrap Beehiiv's official API and add compact insight commands for the workflows agents ask for most. Start with `insights growth-summary`, then drill into subscribers, sources, posts, fields, referrals, or raw endpoints.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the token and base URL before reading account data.
Find the publication ID used by most Beehiiv endpoints.
Get the compact overview before choosing a narrower workflow.
Narrow source-attribution output for agent context.

troubleshoots.symptom: doctor says auth is not configured
troubleshoots.fix: Run `beehiiv-pp-cli auth set-token YOUR_TOKEN` or set `BEEHIIV_BEARER_AUTH`.

troubleshoots.symptom: a publication command returns an empty result
troubleshoots.fix: Run `beehiiv-pp-cli publications index --agent` and make sure the publication ID starts with `pub_`.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs to inspect Beehiiv publications, subscribers, posts, custom fields, referrals, or workspace identity from a shell-safe interface. Prefer `insights` commands for compact reasoning surfaces and raw endpoint commands when you need exact Beehiiv API parity.

recipes.title: Start with publication context
recipes.explanation: Returns the publication IDs needed for subscriber, post, field, and referral workflows.

recipes.title: Get a compact growth overview
recipes.explanation: Combines the main health signals into one response.

recipes.title: Inspect acquisition sources
recipes.explanation: Shows the channel counts without dumping every subscriber row.

recipes.title: Look up one subscriber
recipes.explanation: Uses the single-record lookup when broad subscriber lists are unnecessary.
